Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First let me understand the current model 1 Create Purchase Requisition Approve Purchase Requisition parallel block Close Case 2 The parallel block allows Create PO Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO Pay Invoice all to happen in any order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The issues with this model 1 Pay Invoice before Receive Goods This is a major violation You cannot pay an invoice before receiving goods 2 Pay Invoice before Receive Invoice Cannot pay an invoice you have not received 3 Match Invoice to PO before Receive Invoice Cannot match an invoice you have not received 4 Pay Invoice before Match Invoice to PO Should match before paying 5 Receive Goods before Create PO Should not receive goods before the PO exists 6 Create PO before Approve Requisition This might be blocked by the sequence but the parallel block starts too early

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The correct Procure to Pay sequence should be 1 Create Purchase Requisition 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Create Purchase Order 4 Receive Goods 5 Receive Invoice 6 Match Invoice to PO 7 Pay Invoice 8 Close Case

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: All of these should be in strict sequence for a basic procure to pay process There is no legitimate parallel execution here each step depends on the previous one

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me create the fixed model Everything in a single SEQUENCE operator All 8 activities in the correct order This ensures No paying before receiving No matching before receiving invoice No receiving goods before PO creation Proper chronological order throughout
